name: Poisson regression and non-normal loss (scikit-learn)
description: Scikit-learn tutorial demonstrating log-linear Poisson regression for insurance claim frequency prediction using the French Motor TPL dataset. Compares Ridge, PoissonRegressor, and HistGradientBoosting with Poisson loss. Verified working.

name: Tweedie regression on insurance claims (scikit-learn)
description: Scikit-learn tutorial showing Poisson, Gamma, and Tweedie regression on French Motor TPL claims. Covers two-step frequency-severity product model vs single Tweedie model for pure premium estimation. Verified working.

name: Generalized Linear Models - scikit-learn User Guide
description: Official scikit-learn documentation covering GLM theory, supported distributions (Normal, Poisson, Gamma, Inverse Gaussian, Tweedie), TweedieRegressor API, and link functions. Verified working.

name: French Motor Claims Datasets freMTPL2freq (Kaggle)
description: Kaggle dataset with 677,991 French motor third-party liability policies. Features include driver age, vehicle characteristics, bonus-malus, region. Standard benchmark for insurance GLM tutorials. Verified working.

name: CAS Monograph: Generalized Linear Models for Insurance Ratemaking
description: Comprehensive CAS monograph by Goldburd, Khare, and Tevet covering GLMs specifically for P&C insurance ratemaking. Covers model building, variable selection, diagnostics, and practical implementation. Canonical actuarial reference.

name: Loss Data Analytics - Open Actuarial Textbook (GLM Chapters)
description: Free open-source actuarial textbook with chapters on GLMs for frequency and severity modeling, including Poisson regression, Gamma regression, and model selection. Interactive R examples throughout.

name: Freakonometrics - Arthur Charpentier's Blog (GLM tag)
description: Blog posts by Arthur Charpentier (professor of actuarial science) covering GLMs, Poisson regression, overdispersion, Tweedie models, and insurance pricing with R examples.

name: statsmodels GLM Documentation
description: Official statsmodels documentation for Generalized Linear Models in Python. Covers families (Gaussian, Binomial, Poisson, Gamma, Tweedie, etc.), link functions, and model fitting with examples.

name: An Introduction to Statistical Learning (ISLR) - Chapter on GLMs
description: Free textbook by James, Witten, Hastie, and Tibshirani. Chapter 4 covers GLMs including logistic regression and Poisson regression with R labs. Widely used in actuarial education.

name: Predictive Modeling Applications in Actuarial Science (Frees, Derrig, Meyers)
description: Online companion to the Cambridge University Press textbook on predictive modeling in actuarial science. Volume 1 covers GLMs for insurance data with R code and datasets.

name: Computational Actuarial Science with R (Chapter on GLMs)
description: Book edited by Arthur Charpentier with dedicated chapters on GLMs for non-life insurance pricing, including Poisson, negative binomial, Gamma, and Tweedie regression in R.

name: Pricing Game Datasets (R package CASdatasets)
description: R package by Dutang and Charpentier containing standard actuarial datasets (freMTPL, workercomp, etc.) commonly used in GLM insurance pricing tutorials and competitions.

name: GLM Practitioner's Guide - Institute and Faculty of Actuaries
description: IFoA resource for actuaries covering practical aspects of GLM implementation in insurance pricing, including model selection, validation, and regulatory considerations.

name: Tweedie Distribution Tutorial (Wikipedia)
description: Comprehensive reference on the Tweedie distribution family, including the compound Poisson-Gamma special case (power 1-2) used in insurance pure premium modeling.

name: sklearn PoissonRegressor API Reference
description: API documentation for scikit-learn's PoissonRegressor with parameters, examples, and links to related GLM estimators (GammaRegressor, TweedieRegressor).

name: sklearn TweedieRegressor API Reference
description: API documentation for scikit-learn's TweedieRegressor covering the power parameter (0=Normal, 1=Poisson, 1-2=Compound Poisson-Gamma, 2=Gamma, 3=Inverse Gaussian).

name: sklearn GammaRegressor API Reference
description: API documentation for scikit-learn's GammaRegressor, used for modeling strictly positive continuous targets like insurance claim severity.

name: R glm() Function Documentation
description: Official R documentation for the glm() function covering family specifications (gaussian, binomial, poisson, Gamma, inverse.gaussian, quasi), link functions, and fitting options.

name: CRAN Task View: Actuarial Science
description: Curated list of R packages relevant to actuarial science, including GLM-related packages for pricing (statmod, tweedie, MASS), loss reserving, and ratemaking.

name: R tweedie Package
description: R package by Peter Dunn for Tweedie exponential family models. Provides density, distribution, and quantile functions for the Tweedie distribution used in insurance pricing.

name: Actuarial Data Science Tutorials (Swiss Association of Actuaries)
description: Collection of tutorials from the Swiss Association of Actuaries covering machine learning and GLMs in insurance, including frequency-severity modeling and neural network extensions of GLMs.

name: Mario Wuthrich - Statistical Foundations of Actuarial Learning and its Applications
description: Comprehensive open-access textbook by Mario Wuthrich covering GLMs, neural networks, and modern actuarial pricing methods. Includes Poisson, Gamma, and Tweedie GLMs with R code.

name: French Motor Third-Party Liability (freMTPL) Tutorial by Lorentzen and Mayer
description: The scikit-learn Tweedie tutorial was inspired by an R tutorial by Lorentzen and Mayer demonstrating frequency-severity decomposition on the freMTPL2 dataset.

name: Databricks Insurance Lakehouse Solution Accelerator
description: Databricks solution accelerator for insurance analytics, demonstrating how to build pricing models including GLMs on the Databricks platform with PySpark and MLflow.

name: h2o GLM Documentation for Insurance
description: H2O.ai documentation for their distributed GLM implementation supporting Tweedie, Poisson, and Gamma families. Includes insurance pricing examples and parameter tuning guidance.

name: Noll, Salzmann, Wuthrich - Case Study: French Motor Third-Party Liability Claims
description: Influential paper comparing classical actuarial GLMs with machine learning methods on the French MTPL dataset. Demonstrates Poisson and Gamma GLMs as baselines, with neural network and boosting extensions.

name: Insurance Pricing with GLMs - Towards Data Science / Medium
description: Collection of Medium/TDS articles on insurance analytics including GLM-based pricing, frequency-severity modeling, and Tweedie regression tutorials in Python.

name: Practical Introduction to Frequency-Severity Models
description: CAS Monograph 5 PDF - comprehensive guide to GLMs for insurance ratemaking covering frequency models (Poisson, negative binomial), severity models (Gamma, log-normal), and pure premium (Tweedie).

name: Jed Frees - Regression Modeling with Actuarial and Financial Applications (GLM Chapter)
description: Free PDF chapter on GLMs from Jed Frees' regression modeling textbook, covering exponential family distributions, link functions, deviance, and actuarial applications.

name: IFoA - Generalized Linear Models: An Introduction
description: Institute and Faculty of Actuaries introductory guide to GLMs covering theory, practical implementation, model diagnostics, and insurance pricing applications.

name: Wuthrich and Buser - Data Analytics for Non-Life Insurance Pricing
description: Open-access lecture notes covering GLMs for non-life insurance pricing including Poisson, negative binomial, and Gamma regression with Swiss motor insurance data.

name: OpenML French MTPL2 Dataset (freMTPL2freq)
description: The French Motor Third-Party Liability frequency dataset on OpenML, used by scikit-learn GLM tutorials. Contains 677,991 policies with claim counts and risk features.

name: xgboost for Insurance Pricing - Comparison with GLM
description: R insurancerating package vignette demonstrating GLM-based insurance pricing workflows including rating factors, burning cost analysis, and comparison with tree-based models.

name: statmod R Package - Tweedie GLM Extensions
description: R package providing Tweedie family for glm(), power variance functions, and other statistical modeling tools commonly used in actuarial GLM work.

name: Coursera: Predictive Modeling and Machine Learning in Actuarial Science
description: Online course covering GLMs and machine learning methods for actuarial applications, including Poisson and Gamma regression for insurance pricing.
